questions.q: The Rajya Sabha is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha is the Council of States, the upper house of Parliament.

questions.q: Within Parliament, the Rajya Sabha is referred to as the:
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha is the upper house of the bicameral Parliament of India.

questions.q: Which feature distinguishes the Rajya Sabha from the Lok Sabha?
options: It can be dissolved by the President
It is a permanent body not subject to dissolution
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution.

questions.q: What fraction of Rajya Sabha members retire every two years?
questions.explanation: One-third of the members of the Rajya Sabha retire every two years.

questions.q: The Rajya Sabha primarily represents the:
questions.explanation: As the Council of States, the Rajya Sabha represents the states and union territories.

questions.q: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President of India is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: What is the term of office for a member of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: A member of the Rajya Sabha is elected for a term of six years.

questions.q: Members of the Rajya Sabha from the states are elected by:
options: Elected members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: State representatives in the Rajya Sabha are elected by the elected members of the state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: Some members of the Rajya Sabha are nominated by the President for their distinction in fields such as:
options: Literature, science, art and social service
questions.explanation: The President nominates members having special knowledge or practical experience in literature, science, art, and social service.

questions.q: Who presides over the Rajya Sabha in the absence of the Chairman?
questions.explanation: The Deputy Chairman presides over the Rajya Sabha in the absence of the Chairman.

questions.q: What is the minimum age to be eligible for membership of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 30 years of age to be eligible for the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The maximum strength of the Rajya Sabha provided in the Constitution is:
questions.explanation: The Constitution fixes the maximum strength of the Rajya Sabha at 250 members.

questions.q: Of the 250 maximum members, how many are nominated by the President?
questions.explanation: Up to twelve members are nominated by the President for their special knowledge or experience.

questions.q: State representatives are elected to the Rajya Sabha through which method?
options: Proportional representation by single transferable vote
questions.explanation: Members are elected by proportional representation through the single transferable vote system.

questions.q: The composition of the Rajya Sabha is governed mainly by which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 80 deals with the composition of the Council of States, the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The allocation of Rajya Sabha seats to the states is set out in which Schedule of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Schedule allocates seats in the Rajya Sabha to the states and union territories.

questions.q: Which power is exclusive to the Rajya Sabha and not shared with the Lok Sabha?
options: Empowering Parliament to legislate on a State List subject
questions.explanation: Under Article 249, only the Rajya Sabha can pass a resolution enabling Parliament to legislate on a State List subject in the national interest.

questions.q: The first sitting of the Rajya Sabha was held in which year?
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha was constituted in 1952, holding its first sitting that year.

questions.q: A member nominated to the Rajya Sabha by the President is not entitled to vote in the election of which office?
questions.explanation: Nominated members of the Rajya Sabha do not take part in the election of the President but do participate in the election of the Vice-President.

questions.q: Because one-third of members retire every two years, the Rajya Sabha as a body is described as:
questions.explanation: Continuous partial renewal makes the Rajya Sabha a continuing or permanent House that is never wholly dissolved.

questions.q: Who can remove the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
options: A resolution of the Rajya Sabha by majority of all then members
questions.explanation: The Deputy Chairman may be removed by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.q: The Rajya Sabha can authorise Parliament to create new All India Services under which Article?
questions.explanation: Under Article 312, the Rajya Sabha may pass a resolution enabling Parliament to create new All India Services.

questions.q: A resolution under Article 249 enabling Parliament to legislate on a State List subject must be passed by what majority in the Rajya Sabha?
options: Two-thirds of members present and voting
An absolute majority of total membership
questions.explanation: Such a resolution requires support of not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting.

questions.q: A resolution passed under Article 249 remains in force for a period not exceeding:
questions.explanation: A resolution under Article 249 stays in force for up to one year and may be renewed.

questions.q: In a joint sitting of both Houses, the Rajya Sabha's relative influence is limited because:
options: The Lok Sabha is the larger House
It cannot vote in joint sittings
questions.explanation: Because the Lok Sabha is numerically larger, its members predominate when a joint sitting votes on a disputed bill.

questions.q: With respect to a money bill, the Rajya Sabha may:
options: Only make recommendations within fourteen days
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha can only recommend changes to a money bill and must return it within fourteen days; the Lok Sabha may accept or reject the recommendations.

questions.q: A requirement that a Rajya Sabha candidate be ordinarily resident in the state they seek to represent was removed by an amendment to which law in 2003?
options: The Representation of the People Act
questions.explanation: The domicile or residency requirement was removed through a 2003 amendment to the Representation of the People Act.

questions.q: The Chairman of the Rajya Sabha may be removed only when a resolution to remove the Vice-President is:
options: Initiated in the Rajya Sabha and agreed to by the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Vice-President, who is ex-officio Chairman, can be removed by a Rajya Sabha resolution agreed to by the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: When a motion for the removal of the Chairman is under consideration, the sitting is presided over by:
options: The Deputy Chairman or another member, not the Chairman
questions.explanation: The Chairman does not preside while a resolution for their own removal is being considered.

questions.q: Seats in the Rajya Sabha are allocated to states broadly in proportion to their:
questions.explanation: Seat allocation in the Fourth Schedule is based broadly on the population of each state, with smaller states given proportionally more weight.
